name: Coevorden (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: the edging of the cross and the castle

description: A red field divided by a centred blue cross fimbriated in yellow, charged at the centre with a golden castle tower.
history: Flag of the municipality of Coevorden in the south-east of the province of Drenthe. Coevorden is a historic fortress town, and the castle tower recalls its role as a stronghold; the charges derive from the municipal arms.

features.role: a centred blue cross edged in yellow, reaching all four edges
